Copyright
• There are some file-sharing websites that specialise in buying and selling
 academic work to and from university students.

• If you upload your original work to these websites, and if another student
 downloads and presents it as their own either wholly or partially, you
 might be found guilty of collusion — even years after graduation.

• These file-sharing websites may also accept purchase of course
 materials, such as copies of lecture slides and tutorial handouts. By law,
 the copyright on course materials, developed by UNSW staff in the
 course of their employment, belongs to UNSW. It constitutes copyright
 infringement, if not academic misconduct, to trade these materials.

Machine Learning for NLP
• Natural Language Processing (NLP) is an interdisciplinary field of
   ⎻ Statistics
   ⎻ Computer Science (Artificial Intelligence)
   ⎻ Linguistics

• Research on the interaction between
   ⎻ Human – Human language (text & speech)
   ⎻ Computer – Computational representation

                                                    Source: revolveai
 Example of ML Model in NLP: Topic   9

Modeling
• Aims to analyze documents to
  find the underlying topics
  (contexts) of those documents
• Commonly known algorithm:
  Latent Dirichlet Allocation
  (LDA)
• Applications
    ‐ Market Research and
      Trend Analysis
    ‐ Legal Document Analysis
    ‐…
 Past: How did Voice Assistants                                                     10

Work?
• Speech Recognition: converts the audio input into text

• Intent Recognition: identifies the user's intent

• Query Processing: converts the intent into a structured query that can be used
 to retrieve relevant information or perform specific actions

• Task Execution: executes the task associated with the intent, such as turning
 on smart home devices, fetching information from the internet

• Response Generation: speaks the response aloud to the user or present it as
 text on the screen
 New Era: Pre-trained Large Language                                             11

Models
Large-scale language models (e.g., Copilot or GPT) trained on vast corpora of
text, leading to breakthroughs in many NLP tasks below, through transfer
learning.
• Question Answering: Creating systems that can understand natural language
  questions and provide relevant and accurate answers

• Sentiment Analysis: Analyzing text to determine the sentiment or emotion
 expressed by the writer, which has applications in social media monitoring,
 customer feedback analysis, and market research.

Python Fundamentals 3
Data structures
• Tuple: A tuple is an immutable sequence in Python. Once a tuple is created, its
  elements cannot be changed, added, or removed.

• List: A list is a mutable, ordered sequence of elements. Elements in a list can be
 of different types and can be modified after creation.

• Set: A set is an unordered collection of unique elements. Sets are mutable, but
 elements must be immutable and unique.

• Dictionary: A dictionary is a mutable, unordered collection of key-value pairs.
 Keys must be unique and immutable (e.g., strings, numbers, tuples), but values
 can be of any type.
                                                                                                                                                            18
Comparison of Data Structures

                                       Mutable                    Ordered                   Indexing                  Duplicate Data

          Tuple                               ×
            List

            Set                                                          ×                          ×                               ×
     Dictionary                                                               1                         1
                                                                                                                                    ×
1 Since Python 3.7, dictionaries are order-preserving, that is they now behave like collections.OrderedDicts. There is no dedicated method to index into
keys and values of the dictionary

Final Exam
• Date and time: Tuesday 9rd December 2024, 9:45 – 12:00 pm (AEST)
• Duration: 2-hour writing time + 15 minutes reading time
• Venue: UNSW Campus
• Format: Multiple-choice questions (80%) and coding (20%) using Inspera Safe Mode
• Content: Lecture, lab, individual assignment, group assignment, and quizzes, except
  the Interactive Widgets in Week 7, File Operations about JSON files in Week 8.
• What you have to bring: Your laptop/macbook + power cable. Bring your physical
  UNSW Student ID card. If you don’t have one, a driver’s license or passport (physical
  copy only) is also accepted.
• No course-related materials is allowed during the exam
• Please click this website for further information related to what you can bring to the exam
  room: https://www.student.unsw.edu.au/exam/checklist
                                                                             26
Final Exam Format
• Total number of questions to be answered – 36.
  ⎻ Multiple-Choice Questions (with only one correct answer) – 44 Marks
     • Each question is worth 2 marks. 22 questions.
  ⎻ Multiple-Choice Questions (with one or more correct answers) – 36
    marks
     • Each question is worth 3 marks. 12 questions.
  ⎻ Coding Questions – 20 marks
     • One question worth 15 marks, the other worth 5 marks. 2 questions.

• Instructions
   ⎻ Each question is worth 3 marks. Each question may have one or
     more correct answers. For each question,
       • Each question has four options.
       • If you select all correct answer(s), 3 marks are awarded.
       • If you select some but not all correct answer(s), partial marks are awarded.
       • If you choose an incorrect answer, some marks will be deducted.
       • The minimum score is capped at 0 mark. In other words, even though you
         select all wrong answers, the score is 0 and never be negative



If you are NOT sure the correct answer(s), please choose only one answer.

answers, the score is 0 and never be negative.




                                What if choosing some correct answer(s) and/or a wrong answer?
                                • Choosing A and B – You will get 2 marks.
                                • Choosing A, B and C – You will get 0.5 marks.
                                • Choosing A and C – You will get -0.5 marks, but the minimum cap
                                  for each question is 0. So, you will get 0.




If you are NOT sure the correct answer(s), please choose only one answer.

Coding – Inspera
                                                                         41
Coding
• Creating a Pandas Series with 10 values, initiating all values as 0
• Replace the third element with 86.
• Print the first four elements in this series.
                                                                         42
Coding – Solution
• Creating a Pandas Series with 10 values, initiating all values as 0
• Replace the third element with 86.
• Print the first four elements in this series.

   import pandas as pd
   s = pd.Series(0, index = range(10))
   s[2] = 86
   print(s[:4])
 Inspera


          43
 Inspera
• Inspera is the examination platform you will use.
• You will use Safe Exam Browser (SEB) for your exam: Inspera
  will stop you from accessing other applications and/or
  websites on your laptop while SEB is running. This will
  ensure that you cannot look at your notes or access the
  websites.
• You must download and install the SEB application. You can
  access this website to download Inspera and test (discuss on
  the next slide) prior to the examination if you have not already
  done so:
       • Inspera: Information for Students | UNSW Current
         Students
 Student Practice Test
• The advice we got from the Inspera team is for all of you to complete
  the Inspera Student Practice Test
  (https://unsw.inspera.com/student#list/112563919). This is an open test that
  any UNSW student can take to check whether they have successfully
  downloaded SEB. This Practice Test will also help students prepare
  their laptops for opening a test in SEB. It is your responsibility to make
  sure the SEB is downloaded and works before coming to class. If you
  have any questions, please go to see UNSW IT walk-in services
  (Contact Us | UNSW IT | UNSW Sydney).
• You can find the generic password on: https://www.student.unsw.edu.au/exams/inspera/on-
 campus




• My recommendation is to have a test on SEB again on Monday,
  08, December, the day before the exam, to ensure everything is
  working.
 Updates and Upgrades
• Both Windows and Mac have regular updates and upgrades

• Do not trigger updates or upgrades two days before the
  examination to ensure the settings are not modified. I believe
  this might be one of the causes for some students who had
  temporary access issues in the second quiz

• If you did, then you should perform the Student Practice Test
  (https://unsw.inspera.com/student#list/112563919) again
 Last Remark
• It is your responsibility to double-check everything before and
  during the exam. We will not accept unreasonable or
  insubstantial excuses, such as claiming that a laptop battery ran
  out during an exam.
• Read the instructions carefully before attempting the questions
• Remember, the end time of the exam is controlled by Inspera, it
  will stop automatically when time is up. So, you must keep an
  eye on your time.

• Lastly, Good luck!
